info.title: GO1 API
info.description: GO1 API for online learning platform. Includes OAuth 2.0 auth, content management, enrollments, users.

get.description: This endpoint should be hit by the end user's browser. It will start the authentication and authorization flows.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Client ID of the application. If `new_client` is set, this can be any string that doesn't match an existing `client_id`.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The URL to redirect to after the user authorizes your application. It must match your client `redirect_uri`.

parameters.name: redirect
parameters.description:
If set to `false`, the user will not be redirected at the end of the flow, but instead the credentials will be presented on a new page.

Can only be used in conjunction with `response_type=code`.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: A list of space separated permissions that will be requested to the user.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: A state string that'll be sent back to the `redirect_uri`.

parameters.name: new_client
parameters.description: When set it will create a new OAuth client if the given `client_id` doesn't exist. The value should be the client name. The generated `client_id` and `client_secret` will be appended to the `redirect_uri` as part of the query string or fragment parameters. Only letters, digits and punctuation characters are allowed.

parameters.name: creation_path
parameters.description:
If set, the value must be a creation path for an existing preset.
Refer to presets in the `#onboard2` service for possible values.

parameters.name: partner_portal_id
parameters.description: If set, the value must be the ID of an existing distribution partner portal.

parameters.name: one_time_token
parameters.description: If you have a user's one time token, you can skip the login form (username & password) by passing the token in the url

200.description: An HTML page asking the User to grant access to your application.

get.description: You can use this public key to validate an access token has been issued by this service. This is the preferred method as it can work offline provided you store the public key. Please make sure to respect the caching TTL and headers when doing so.

post.description: It retrieves an access token for all grant types except _implicit_.

client_secret.description: The application client secret. Required, except if your application does not have one.

code.description: Required when grant_type is authorization_code. Do not pass otherwise.

refresh_token.description: Required when grant_type is refresh_token. Do not pass otherwise.

jwt.description: Required when grant_type is go1_jwt. Do not pass otherwise.

portal_name.description: Required when your application is public and you're using a go1_jwt or password grant_type. Do not pass otherwise.

redirect_uri.description: The application redirect_uri. Required when grant_type is authorization_code. For security purposes it needs to match the one registered in the application.

client_secret.description: The application client secret. Required, except if your application does not have one.

code.description: Required when grant_type is authorization_code. Do not pass otherwise.

refresh_token.description: Required when grant_type is refresh_token. Do not pass otherwise.

jwt.description: Required when grant_type is go1_jwt. Do not pass otherwise.

portal_name.description: Required when your application is public and you're using a go1_jwt or password grant_type. Do not pass otherwise.

redirect_uri.description: The application redirect_uri. Required when grant_type is authorization_code. For security purposes it needs to match the one registered in the application.

get.description: Checks whether the access token is valid and hasn't been revoked and returns back some information. You should only need to use it for debugging purposes or inside a resource server.

403.description: Invalid access token or inactive client

post.summary: Revoke an access token for the calling user.
post.description: Checks whether the access token is valid and revokes it.

get.summary: Get a list of OAuth clients
get.description: Retrieves a list of OAuth client applications owned by the user, or associated with a specific portal. Only applications with special permissions are allowed to use this endpoint.

parameters.name: portal_name
parameters.description: When given it'll retrieve all OAuth clients registered to that portal. Omit for public clients.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Return N results maximum. Defaults to 10, and must be <= 50

post.description: Creates a new OAuth client application. Only applications with special permissions are allowed to use this endpoint.

portal_name.description: When provided it'll create a private client. Omit for public clients.

redirect_uri.description: The fully qualified URL that will be used to send responses in the authorization flow.

client_name.description: The OAuth client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

portal_name.description: When provided it'll create a private client. Omit for public clients.

redirect_uri.description: The fully qualified URL that will be used to send responses in the authorization flow.

client_name.description: The OAuth client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

redirect_uri.description: The URL to redirect to after the user authorizes your application. It must match your client redirect_uri.

client_name.description: The client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

redirect_uri.description: The URL to redirect to after the user authorizes your application. It must match your client redirect_uri.

client_name.description: The client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

post.description: Generates a new random secret for the OAuth client. You need an application access token, thus obtained through client_credentials flow.

get.description: Lists and filters existing OAuth applications for Go1 internal administrative purposes

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Return N results maximum. Defaults to 10, and must be <= 50

parameters.name: portal_name
parameters.description: Portal name must start with the given string

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Application must belong to the user id

parameters.name: client_name
parameters.description: Relevance text search is done over the client application name

put.description: Allows configuring OAuth applications for Go1 internal administrative purposes

redirect_uri.description: The URL to redirect to after the user authorizes your application. It must match your client redirect_uri.

client_name.description: The client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

login_user.description: Whether it can generate one time login links for users

admin_login_only.description: Whether only users with administrator role can login, or everyone can

admin_apps.description: Whether the application is allowed to change details of other applications

password_grant.description: Whether the application can send username and password directly to the

post.summary: Store a new custom attribute mapping for a particular config_id.
post.description: Validates and stores custom attributes as entry for the entity - there exists a constraint so that there can be no two entries with the same combination of config_id + name

requestBody.description: type can be one of 'attr' or 'const', where the value field corresponds to the existing provided attributes or any value as is the case when type is const

201.description: The new custom attribute mapping was created and stored represented by the id of that entry

400.description: Invalid payload or unable to process the custom attribute mapping or config_id + name combination already exists

403.description: User does not have Admin on Accounts

404.description: config_id passed within the payload does not exist

500.description: Unable to store the custom attribute mapping.

get.summary: Retrieve a custom attribute mapping for a particular id.
get.description: Uses the provided `id` to locate and return a custom attribute mapping.

200.description: OK. A `custom_atribute_mapping` object is provided in the response.

example.name: exampleCustomAttrOne
example.desc: This example attribute is a constant and will always have a value of true

403.description: User does not have Admin on Accounts

404.description: id passed within the payload does not exist

500.description: Internal Server error. Unable to retrieve the custom attribute mapping.

patch.summary: Updates (partial modification) an existing custom attribute mapping for a given entry.
patch.description: Validates and updates field(s) of a custom attribute entry - there exists a constraint so that there can be no two entries with the same combination of config_id + name

requestBody.description: type can be one of 'attr' or 'const', where the value field corresponds to the existing provided attributes or any value as is the case when type is const

200.description: The custom attribute mapping was updated - the entry is returned

example.name: exampleCustomAttrOne
example.desc: This example attribute is a constant and will always have a value of true

400.description: Invalid payload or unable to process the custom attribute mapping or config_id + name combination already exists

403.description: User does not have Admin on Accounts

404.description: url param id does not exist or config_id passed within the payload does not exist

500.description: Unable to store the custom attribute mapping.

get.summary: Retrieve a collection of `custom_atribute_mapping` objects for a particular config_id.
get.description: Uses the provided `config_id` to locate and return a collection of `custom_atribute_mapping` objects for a particular config_id.

200.description: OK. A collection of `custom_atribute_mapping` objects is provided in the response.

example.name: customAttrTwo
example.desc: This customm attribute example will always have a value of 1

example.name: customAttrThree
example.desc: This customm attribute example will always have a value of 1

403.description: User does not have Admin on Accounts

404.description: config_id passed within the payload does not exist

500.description: Internal Server error. Unable to retrieve the custom attribute mapping.

delete.summary: Deletes a custom attribute mapping entry
delete.description: Deletes a custom attribute mapping entry

204.description: The custom attribute mapping was deleted succcessfully

403.description: User does not have Admin on Accounts

404.description: the custom_attribute_mapping_id value does not exist

get.summary: Retrieves the metadata for the entity
get.description: Retrieves the metadata for the provided entity_id. Note that only one of id or entity_id needs to be provided.

parameters.name: portal_id
parameters.description: the id of the portal the metadata is associated with.

200.description: OK. The metadata xml is provided in the response body.

404.description: Not found. The requested entry could not be found.

put.description: Validates and updates the metadata entry for an existing entity and portal. Note that only one of id or entity_id needs to be provided.

parameters.name: portal_id
parameters.description: the id of the portal the metadata is associated with.

400.description: Bad request. The entry was not able to be stored.

401.description: User is not authorized to create metadata entries.

422.description: Unable to process or store the metadata.

post.description: Validates and stores the metadata entry for the entity.

parameters.name: portal_id
parameters.description: the id of the portal the metadata is associated with.

201.description: The new entry was created and stored.

400.description: Bad request. The entry was not able to be stored.

401.description: User is not authorized to create metadata entries.

422.description: Unable to process or store the metadata.

parameters.description: User id to filter enrolments. Use comma seperated list to get a list of enrolments for multiple users.
parameters.name: user_id

get.description: Get list of enrolments for a particular user in the portal

201.description: A new enrolment has been created

get.summary: Get a (filtered) list of learning objects

get.description:
Detailed information about a learning object (LO).

The schema of the object returned depends on the LO type.
Courses contain modules under `items`, and modules contain learning items under `items`.
Learning items themselves have no `items`.

200.description: A SCORM package in a zip file

get.description: Download learning object as a SCORM package.
get.summary: Download learning object as a SCORM package

get.description: Retrieves a list of users filtered by the given search criteria

post.description: Will create a new user. An email will be sent to the user for them to login and set their password.

formData_redirectUri.description: The URL to redirect to after the user authorizes your application. It must match your client redirect_uri.

formData_clientName.description: The client name. It'll be shown to the users when authenticating.

hint.description: A user friendly description of what might have gone wrong

OAuthException.description: An OAuth request that could not be completed successfully

OAuthValidate.description: Information extracted from the access token itself to facilitate consumption of the data by resource servers

login_user.description: Whether it can generate one time login links for users

admin_login_only.description: Whether only users with administrator role can login, or everyone can

admin_apps.description: Whether the application is allowed to change details of other applications

password_grant.description: Whether the application can send username and password directly to the

allOf.description: Go1 Staff view of a client

lo.description: An array of learning object IDs to be added.

BaseLO.description: A base learning object with no children items.

created_time.description: Created date and time, according to RFC 3339

description.example: This is a longer description of the learning object.

updated_time.description: Updated date and time, according to RFC 3339

licenses.description: The number of licenses. -1 means unlimited.

allOf.description: A course excluding its children items.
A course including its children items.

parent_lo_id.description: Either the course or module learning object id.

lo_id.description: Learning object id to enrol into.

parent_lo_id.description: Either the course or module learning object id.

allOf.description: A module including its children items.

lo.description: An array of learning object IDs to be removed.

created.description: Date time of when the user account was created

updated.description: Date time of when the user account was last updated

last_login.description: Last time the user logged in

roles.description: List of roles associated with the user

status.description: When false the user is blocked
